name: Iron Works Golf Course
about: Iron Works Golf Course is an executive golf course in Duanesburg, New York, featuring a 9-hole layout designed by David Schaffer in 2003. The course is characterized by small greens with some slopes, requiring accurate tee shots to shoot par, and includes two par 4 holes for added depth. It is a public venue that does not require tee times and costs $15 to play, making it accessible for golfers of all skill levels, particularly beginners looking to learn the game and experienced players seeking a relaxed environment for short game practice or friendly competition.
why_golfers_like_it: Beginner-friendly environment with open arms for new golfers
Relaxing layout for experienced players' short game practice

food_and_drink.notes: No food or beverage services mentioned

faq.question: Do I need my own clubs?
faq.answer: The website does not specify club rental options, so it is recommended to bring your own clubs.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: There is no mention of food or beverage services, so outside food and drinks may be permitted, but this is not confirmed.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The course is a 9-hole executive course, so playing 18 holes would typically take 2-3 hours depending on pace of play.

faq.question: Is the course kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the course is beginner-friendly and welcomes new golfers, making it suitable for juniors and families.
